metadata.title: The Class-Size Paradox
metadata.notes: Calling Bullshit (Vox Style), film three. Worked example is the book's own, VERIFIED VERBATIM: 20 classes of 20 + 4 of 200; mean 50; 800 of 1,200 students in the giant classes; experienced mean 140. Color law: navy = students, ink = classrooms, golden highlighter = the brochure number (once, A10 context). Exclusions: no weighted-mean formula on screen, no US News subplot, no friendship-paradox. Outro pads A12.
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

skin_warnings: NO RENDERABLE BEATS: no beat carries a shot.remotion.pattern or Manim annotation — the pipeline will render NOTHING and every slot becomes a YOU-slate. The sheet was authored without the scene contract.
A01: palette=claude but the cold open is 'un-annotated' — COLD OPEN LAW wants ClaudeComposerAsk
A12: palette=claude but the outro is 'un-annotated' — OUTRO LAW wants ClaudeTitleOutro

beats.narration_text: This is Liam, in for Bear. Your university says the average class is fifty students. It feels more like a hundred and forty. Here's the trick: both numbers are true.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Your university says the average class is fifty students. It feels more like a hundred and forty. Here's the trick: both numbers are true.

shot.note: cold open — packed lecture hall from the back row, flat light; shot.focus mid-hall

build.suggested: suggested prompt: [PHOTO] the hall that feels like 140

beats.narration_text: Two averages, honestly computed, wildly different. This is the story of how a university tells the truth and misleads you anyway.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Two averages, honestly computed, wildly different. This is the story of how a university tells the truth and misleads you anyway.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Two averages, honestly computed, wildly different. This is the story of how a university tells the truth and misleads you anyway.

beats.narration_text: Picture a tiny college: twenty-four classes. Twenty of them hold twenty students each. Four are monsters, with two hundred students apiece.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Picture a tiny college: twenty-four classes. Twenty of them hold twenty students each. Four are monsters, with two hundred students apiece.

viz.note: grid of 24 classroom squares: 20 small, 4 large — the book's own worked example, verified verbatim

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Picture a tiny college: twenty-four classes. Twenty of them hold twenty students each. Four are monsters, with two hundred students apiece.

beats.narration_text: Average over the classes, and the administrator is right: twenty-four classrooms, mean size fifty. That's the number in the brochure.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Average over the classes, and the administrator is right: twenty-four classrooms, mean size fifty. That's the number in the brochure.

viz.note: counter averages over classrooms → 50; golden highlighter on the 50 (the brochure number — the pen)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Average over the classes, and the administrator is right: twenty-four classrooms, mean size fifty. That's the number in the brochure.

beats.narration_text: Now ask the other question: don't average the classrooms. Average the students' experience.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Now ask the other question: don't average the classrooms. Average the students' experience.

card.title: AVERAGE THE STUDENTS, NOT THE CLASSES

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Now ask the other question: don't average the classrooms. Average the students' experience.

beats.narration_text: Drop into this school as a random student, and odds are you land in a monster: two-thirds of all students are sitting in the four giant lectures.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Drop into this school as a random student, and odds are you land in a monster: two-thirds of all students are sitting in the four giant lectures.

viz.note: a navy student-square drops repeatedly; 800 of 1,200 land in the four big rooms (book's figures)

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Drop into this school as a random student, and odds are you land in a monster: two-thirds of all students are sitting in the four giant lectures.

beats.narration_text: Average what students actually experience, and the number isn't fifty. It's one hundred and forty.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Average what students actually experience, and the number isn't fifty. It's one hundred and forty.

viz.note: the counter recomputes over students → 140; (400·20 + 800·200)/1,200 = 140, verified

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Average what students actually experience, and the number isn't fifty. It's one hundred and forty.

beats.narration_text: Big classes contain more students, so a random student lands in a big class more often than a random class is big.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Big classes contain more students, so a random student lands in a big class more often than a random class is big.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Big classes contain more students, so a random student lands in a big class more often than a random class is big.

beats.narration_text: Same school, same classes, no lies: the class average is fifty, the student average is one hundred forty. The choice of average is the message.
beats.tts_normalized_text: Same school, same classes, no lies: the class average is fifty, the student average is one hundred forty. The choice of average is the message.

viz.note: both counters side by side over the same campus grid

build.suggested: suggested prompt: Same school, same classes, no lies: the class average is fifty, the student average is one hundred forty. The choice of average is the message.

beats.narration_text: And guess which number makes the brochure.
beats.tts_normalized_text: And guess which number makes the brochure.

shot.note: glossy admissions brochure on a desk, flat light; shot.focus on the cover

beats.narration_text: The same trick makes your flights feel fuller and your gym feel busier — anywhere big groups hold more witnesses, experience outweighs the average.
beats.tts_normalized_text: The same trick makes your flights feel fuller and your gym feel busier — anywhere big groups hold more witnesses, experience outweighs the average.

viz.note: isotype pair: plane rows and gym floor — crowded units hold most of the witnesses

build.suggested: suggested prompt: The same trick makes your flights feel fuller and your gym feel busier — anywhere big groups hold more witnesses, experience outweighs the average.

beats.narration_text: So when someone quotes you an average, ask: averaged over what? The units chosen decide the story told.
beats.tts_normalized_text: So when someone quotes you an average, ask: averaged over what? The units chosen decide the story told.

build.suggested: suggested prompt: So when someone quotes you an average, ask: averaged over what? The units chosen decide the story told.
